stories.title: Thunder Roll Lakers 125-107 in Game 2 — OKC Takes 2-0 Series Lead

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Chet Holmgren: 22 pts, 9 reb, 4 stl, 2 blk.
SGA: 22 pts. Mitchell: 20. McCain: 18.

The No. 1 Thunder don't have a weakness.

OKC leads 2-0. #ThunderUp

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The Thunder outscored the Lakers 32-15 in the 3rd quarter.

WHILE SGA was on the bench.

Oklahoma City's depth is unreal this postseason. #ThunderUp

stories.title: Pistons Lock Down Cavaliers 107-97 — Cade's Clutch 4th Quarter Seals 2-0 Lead

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Cade Cunningham was LOCKED IN tonight.

25 pts, 10 ast, 12 pts in the 4th, 8-8 FT when it mattered.

Pistons lead 2-0. Cade is THAT guy. #DetroitBasketball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Cavs shot 0-for-11 from three in the 4th quarter.

Mitchell had 31. He did everything he could.

Detroit's defense just would not budge. Pistons lead 2-0. #DetroitBasketball

stories.title: Wembanyama and Spurs Obliterate Wolves 133-95 — Minnesota's Worst Playoff Loss Ever

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
133-95.

The Spurs just handed Minnesota its WORST postseason loss in franchise history.

Wembanyama: 19 pts, 15 reb in 26 minutes. He didn't even need the full game. #GoSpursGo

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Minnesota shot 29.8% in the 1st half. Went 2-for-15 from three.

Trailed by 25 at halftime.

Game 3 tonight: 9:30 PM ET on Prime Video. Can the Wolves bounce back at home? #Timberwolves

article.headline: Wembanyama and Spurs Obliterate Wolves 133-95 — Minnesota's Worst Playoff Loss in Franchise History

research.facts:

- **HIGH**: Stephon Castle: 21 points
- **HIGH**: De'Aaron Fox: 16 points
- **HIGH**: Spurs shot 50% from field, 41% from three
- **HIGH**: Minnesota held to 35 points in first half

stories.title: Knicks Survive 76ers Scare 108-102 — Brunson, KAT, OG Power NYK to 2-0 Lead

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
No Embiid. No problem — for the Knicks.

Brunson 26. OG 24. KAT 20/10/7 in 27 foul-plagued minutes.

New York wins 108-102 on a 9-0 closing run. Up 2-0. #NewYorkForever

## Notes / Caveats
- Wembanyama's Game 1 stats (12 blocks, 11 pts, 15 reb) referenced in articles should be marked MEDIUM — only appeared in summarized sources, not cross-referenced with box score
- All Game 2 stats above were consistent across multiple sources (CBS Sports boxscores, NBA.com recaps, beat reporters)
